gloss: (lit., to wade in water or mud)
(lit., exalted is Allah above allowing this to happen) God forbid - an expression invoking Allah to prevent something from happening

deaf; used metaphorically for those who turn a deaf ear to the truth
(lit., to be done with), including decreeing or judging once and for all, finishing or fulfilling, and ending someone's life.
lit., to blindly guess the amount of dates on a palm tree; to lie by guessing blindly.

(for a bone cap to fit perfectly in its socket or for a camel to be fit to carry heavy loads--hence, to be the right fit, to be right, due, justified, established)

gloss: Faith, religion, or way of life.

Question particle used for is/was/are/were/have/has...? and for whether.

(lit., to be wholesome, to submit as a whole in peace)

lit., extreme anger, wrath; to enrage, anger.

(lit., a person with thick muscles who can overpower others)

to be good in essence, value, and appearance

gloss: (lit., to be solid and true, not flimsy)

to give someone time; molten metal left in fire for a while.

(lit., a very tall date palm that towers above all others)

to come upon or find in battle.

(lit., for something to be easy to do because it is small or simple)
(lit., for Allah to set the limit, measure, worth, or fate of something by virtue of His infinite power)
lit., an area of land left with nothing on it; to cease or leave, always preceded by negation lā/lan and followed by ḥattā.
(lit., to anchor); to cause something to stand firm
(lit., to refine gold by melting it in the fire) to test
(lit., to be too far ahead of someone so they cannot catch up); to outrun, miss, escape; inconsistencies or gaps.

lit., to split up into two; to separate a large number of people into two groups; to burst, splitting into two.

root, base, stem (always in reference to trees)

lit., one of the pillars supporting a structure; to rely on or incline towards someone for support.
to be ablaze, to be on fire
to share in, to associate, partner

(lit., from قَطَّ qaṭ-ṭa, to cut--hence a piece or share); share

to humble, humiliate; tame, smooth out

the one covered up in his clothes (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
to fall to one’s doom; killed by falling.

lit., to turn, flip (in the sense that something doesn't have its original form anymore)

lit., neatly spaced teeth; to recite in a measured way.

empty, private space; to be alone; to go leaving an empty space
